primary_type: Open Primary (voters do not need to be party members)

ballotpedia.name: Ballotpedia
ballotpedia.description: Nonpartisan political encyclopedia covering candidates, races, and election data

wuwm_voter_guide.name: WUWM 89.7 FM Milwaukee Public Radio — Voter Guide
wuwm_voter_guide.description: Milwaukee-area public radio voter guide with candidate questionnaire responses

wisconsin_ethics_commission.name: Wisconsin Ethics Commission — Sunshine Campaign Finance
wisconsin_ethics_commission.description: Official Wisconsin state campaign finance reporting system. Contains all state-level candidate finance reports from July 2008 to present.

data_access.csv_download: Available via 'Download Results' button on any search — up to 100,000 rows per download
data_access.note: No public REST API. Data is accessible via web portal with CSV export. Commercial use of data is prohibited under Wis. Stat. § 11.1304(12). Non-commercial research, journalism, and general information use is permitted.

fec_openfec_api.name: Federal Election Commission — OpenFEC API
fec_openfec_api.description: Official FEC REST API for federal candidate and committee campaign finance data. Covers U.S. House and Senate candidates only (not state-level).

data_access.note: Free to use. Commercial restrictions apply to contributor lists. Wisconsin state candidates (Governor, AG, Assembly, Senate, etc.) are NOT in FEC data — use Wisconsin Ethics Commission Sunshine for those.

fec_web_portal.name: FEC Data Portal
fec_web_portal.description: Web-based FEC data explorer for browsing candidates, committees, and filings

pbs_wisconsin.name: PBS Wisconsin
pbs_wisconsin.description: Wisconsin public broadcasting news and election coverage

campaign_finance.instructions: Search by candidate name under 'Registrant Type: State Candidate' to find their committee and finance reports
campaign_finance.note: Governor is a state office — not tracked by FEC. Use Wisconsin Sunshine only.

candidates.name: Mandela Barnes
candidates.background: Lieutenant Governor of Wisconsin (2019–2023), the first Black Wisconsinite to hold the office; former state Assembly representative from Milwaukee; narrowly lost the 2022 U.S. Senate race; announced his run for governor December 2, 2025 and is widely viewed as the Democratic frontrunner
candidates.notes: Entered the race December 2, 2025. Sunshine committee 'Mandela for Wisconsin' still shows his prior Lieutenant Governor registration; gubernatorial campaign finance expected in the July 29, 2026 pre-primary filing.

candidates.name: Joel Brennan
candidates.current_occupation: Former Secretary of the Wisconsin Department of Administration
candidates.background: Former Secretary of the Wisconsin Department of Administration (2019–2023) under Gov. Tony Evers; businessman and politician born 1970

candidates.name: David Crowley
candidates.background: First Black man elected Milwaukee County Executive (since 2020); previously represented a Milwaukee district in the state Assembly
candidates.notes: Withdrew July 8, 2026 and endorsed Rodriguez; re-entered the primary July 18, 2026 after her exit.

candidates.name: Francesca Hong
candidates.background: Wisconsin State Assembly member representing District 76 (Madison, since 2021); former chef and restaurant owner; born November 4, 1988; first Asian American elected to Wisconsin Legislature; single mother
candidates.notes: Republicans spending $2.2M in ads targeting Hong, suggesting they view her as their preferred opponent

candidates.name: Brett Hulsey
candidates.status: Did not file by June 1, 2026 deadline — not on primary ballot
candidates.background: Former state representative from Madison; former Dane County Board member; runs an energy and environmental small business

candidates.name: Tim Jacobson
candidates.background: Registered with Wisconsin Ethics Commission; limited public information available

candidates.name: Missy Hughes
candidates.background: Stepped down from leading the Wisconsin Economic Development Corporation to run for governor; previously an executive in the dairy industry

candidates.name: Sara Rodriguez
candidates.background: Lieutenant Governor of Wisconsin under Gov. Tony Evers; formerly represented portions of Waukesha and Milwaukee counties in the state Assembly; registered nurse; withdrew citing campaign finance reporting issues
candidates.notes: Withdrew July 17, 2026 after discovering campaign finance mismanagement; campaign manager fired over unreported fundraising shortfall.

candidates.name: Kelda Roys
candidates.background: Wisconsin State Senator representing District 26 (since 2021); attorney, small business owner, and former nonprofit executive; born June 24, 1979; previously served in the state Assembly; endorsed by WEAC (teachers union) and former Lt. Gov. Mandela Barnes

candidates.name: Zachary Roper
candidates.status: Did not file by June 1, 2026 deadline — not on primary ballot
candidates.background: Political science student at Carthage College; previously ran for Wisconsin State Assembly (District including Muskego); lost to Chuck Wichgers

candidates.name: Ryan Strnad
candidates.status: Did not file by June 1, 2026 deadline — not on primary ballot
candidates.background: Lives in Mukwonago; left the Republican Party in 2001 to become a Democrat; running to improve the working class, healthcare, and education

candidates.name: Tom Tiffany
candidates.background: U.S. Representative for Wisconsin's 7th Congressional District (2020–present); former Wisconsin State Senator; endorsed by President Donald Trump (January 2026)
key_priorities: Eliminate Gov. Evers' 400-year revenue increase for schools

candidates.name: Andy Manske
candidates.background: Born in Milwaukee, raised in Franklin; has acknowledged having a 'very slim chance to win' against Tiffany; grassroots campaign
key_priorities: Property tax reform for paid-off homeowners

candidates.name: Josh Schoemann
candidates.background: Washington County Executive; veteran; touts record of cutting taxes and reducing government staff
candidates.notes: Dropped out one day after President Trump endorsed Tom Tiffany.

candidates.name: Oliver Carranza
candidates.background: UW-Madison student; running to challenge partisan gridlock; priorities include small businesses, manufacturing, healthcare costs, and public schools

candidates.name: Crystal Harper
candidates.background: Limited public information available; active on social media

candidates.name: Mike Kohn
candidates.background: Registered with Wisconsin Ethics Commission; limited public information available

candidates.name: Korey Streich
candidates.background: Registered with Wisconsin Ethics Commission; limited public information available

races.notes: Wisconsin holds separate gubernatorial and lieutenant gubernatorial primaries; nominees then run together on a single ticket in the general election.

campaign_finance.note: Lt. Governor is a state office — use Wisconsin Sunshine only.

candidates.name: Sarah Godlewski
candidates.background: Former Wisconsin State Treasurer (2019–2023); appointed Secretary of State by Gov. Evers in 2023 after Doug La Follette's retirement; previously ran for U.S. Senate in 2022

campaign_finance.note: Attorney General is a state office — use Wisconsin Sunshine only.

candidates.name: Josh Kaul
candidates.background: Incumbent Wisconsin Attorney General (since 2019); attorney; son of former U.S. Solicitor General Donald Verrilli

candidates.name: Eric Toney
candidates.current_occupation: Fond du Lac County District Attorney
candidates.background: Fond du Lac County District Attorney; previously ran for Attorney General in 2022

races.notes: Incumbent Sarah Godlewski stepped down to run for Lt. Governor. The Republican to receive the most votes in the primary will advance to the general election.

sources.name: Ballotpedia — Wisconsin Secretary of State Election 2026
WUWM Voter Guide — Secretary of State

campaign_finance.note: Secretary of State is a state office — use Wisconsin Sunshine only.

candidates.name: JoCasta Zamarripa
candidates.background: Served a decade in the Wisconsin State Assembly (District 8, Milwaukee); currently in second term on the Milwaukee Common Council; led 'ICE Out of Milwaukee' effort; would be first out LGBTQ+ Constitutional Officer in Wisconsin

candidates.name: Brayden Myer
candidates.background: UW-Green Bay graduate (Magna Cum Laude, finished in 2.25 years); former congressional staffer; Chapter Chair of Manitowoc County Moms for Liberty; founded Manitowoc County Patriots
key_priorities: Abolish Wisconsin Elections Commission; restore SOS oversight of elections

candidates.name: Nathan Pollnow
candidates.background: Second-generation beef farmer; avid hunter and auctioneer; did not respond to WUWM questionnaire
key_priorities: Public registry of rules, pardons, and enacted laws — searchable and free
School trust funds serving all of Wisconsin

candidates.name: Jay Schroeder
candidates.background: Has studied election integrity issues for over 10 years; claims to have discussed Wisconsin election problems with President Trump at Mar-a-Lago
key_priorities: Paper ballots only; eliminate voting machines
Restrict mail-in voting and drop boxes
Proof of citizenship and ID requirement

candidates.name: Cindy Werner
candidates.background: Did not respond to WUWM questionnaire; no campaign website or readily available information; has a public Facebook page

candidates.name: Pete Karas
candidates.background: Former Racine city council member; retired music venue owner; running to break the two-party lock
key_priorities: Reinstate Government Accountability Board for election oversight

races.office_description: Oversees state financial assets; manages $1.6 billion Common School Fund (distributes profits to public schools); chairs Board of Commissioners of Public Lands; manages unclaimed property program

campaign_finance.note: State Treasurer is a state office — use Wisconsin Sunshine only.

candidates.name: Dylan Ray Helmenstine
candidates.background: Former auditor and financial analyst for Wisconsin Medicaid programs; Economics degree and Master's in Public Affairs; appointed by Gov. Evers to Teachers' Retirement Board; elected to school board and village board in rural Wisconsin
key_priorities: Government transparency dashboard for state budget
Protect Common School Trust Fund from political interference
Transparency on voucher school tax diversion

candidates.name: Yee Leng Xiong
candidates.current_occupation: Executive Director at New Beginnings for Refugees; Marathon County Supervisor; D.C. Everest School Board Treasurer
candidates.background: Born in Wausau to refugee parents who fled persecution; Marathon County District 19 Supervisor (since 2016); D.C. Everest Area School Board Treasurer (since 2014); former Village of Weston Trustee (2018–2022); only candidate in the race who has served as treasurer of a public body
key_priorities: Protect and grow $1.3B Common School Fund
Support low-interest Trust Fund Loans for community infrastructure

candidates.name: John Leiber
candidates.background: Incumbent Wisconsin State Treasurer (since 2023); oversaw record $73.5 million distribution from Common School Fund to public schools
key_priorities: Continue smart fiscal management of Common School Fund
Focus on existing duties rather than expanding office powers

races.office: Wisconsin Supreme Court — Rebecca Bradley's Seat
races.notes: Wisconsin Supreme Court elections are nonpartisan. More than two candidates must file for a primary to be held. Term is 10 years.

campaign_finance.note: Judicial candidates file as state candidates with the Wisconsin Ethics Commission.

candidates.name: Chris Taylor
candidates.background: Former Wisconsin State Assembly member representing District 76 (Madison); attorney
